new_classes.label_en: Semi-Sparkling Wine of Fresh Grapes in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 29 10)
new_classes.label_de: Perlwein aus frischen Weintrauben in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2204 29 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes (other than sparkling wine of subheading 2204 10), put up in containers holding more than 2 litres, either in bottles with mushroom stoppers held in place by ties or fastenings, or otherwise put up with an excess pressure due to carbon dioxide in solution of not less than 1 bar but less than 3 bar, measured at a temperature of 20 °C.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) wine of fresh grapes other than subheading 2204 10 (sparkling wine), (2) in containers > 2 L (LargeContainer), (3) semi-sparkling character expressed via excess pressure ≥ 1 bar and < 3 bar at 20 °C (ExcessPressureQuality with decimalRange restrictions on excessPressureBar), and (4) the alternative presentation criterion of bottles with mushroom stoppers (MushroomStopperBottleQuality). A small gap remains: the disjointness restriction uses the 'complement' type referencing SparklingWine with a property IRI (isCarbonated) which is a proxy; the legal text specifies exclusion of subheading 2204 10 specifically (true sparkling wine, excess pressure ≥ 3 bar), which is modelled structurally by the maxExclusive 3.0 bar restriction rather than a formal disjointWith axiom on SparklingWine. Also, the measurement temperature of 20 °C cannot be expressed in the current TBox datatype model. Coverage is assessed at ~0.82.
